author: Grafana Alloy Operator maintainers
tooling: Reachability for Go entries assessed with govulncheck (binary mode); see .vex/README.md

statements.impact_statement: The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. GnuTLS DTLS DoS: the Go binary uses Go's crypto/tls, not GnuTLS.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. GnuTLS DTLS heap overflow: the Go binary uses Go's crypto/tls, not GnuTLS.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. GnuTLS DTLS reordering DoS: the Go binary uses Go's crypto/tls, not GnuTLS.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. GnuTLS NUL-in-username auth bypass: the Go binary uses Go's crypto/tls, not GnuTLS.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. libarchive RAR5 DoS: the Go binary uses Go's archive/tar, not libarchive, and processes no RAR archives.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. libarchive RAR OOB read: the Go binary uses Go's archive/tar, not libarchive, and processes no RAR archives.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. libcap cap_set_file TOCTOU: the operator never shells out to setcap or calls cap_set_file.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. nghttp2 HTTP/2 DoS: the Go binary uses Go's net/http2, not libnghttp2.

The only process in this image is the statically linked, CGO_ENABLED=0 Go helm-operator binary (verified via `go version -m`); it does not dynamically link or invoke this RPM. The package ships in the UBI base filesystem but is never loaded or executed by the operator. OpenSSL PKCS7_verify UAF: the Go binary uses Go's crypto, not openssl-libs.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. SPDY DoS: spdystream is exercised only by client-go exec/attach/port-forward streaming, which this operator never initiates; any SPDY peer would be the trusted API server.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. crypto/x509 chain-building DoS requires parsing an attacker-supplied certificate chain; the only certificates verified are the API server's, issued by the cluster CA.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. crypto/x509 policy-validation DoS requires an attacker-supplied certificate chain; the only certificates verified are the API server's, issued by the cluster CA.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. crypto/tls TLS 1.3 KeyUpdate DoS requires a malicious TLS peer; the operator's only TLS peer is the trusted API server.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. net DNS CNAME crash requires a malicious DNS response; resolution is served by trusted in-cluster CoreDNS for cluster service names.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. x/net HTTP/2 transport infinite loop requires connecting to a malicious HTTP/2 server; the operator's HTTP/2 client talks only to the trusted API server.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. net/mail quadratic-parse DoS requires untrusted RFC 5322 message input; the operator parses no email/MIME.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. html/template XSS only matters when output is served as HTML to a browser; rendered output is Kubernetes YAML manifests, never HTML.
Panic in net.Dial/LookupPort on a NUL byte affects only GOOS=windows; this image is GOOS=linux/amd64, so the vulnerable code path cannot execute.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. net/mail quadratic-parse DoS requires untrusted RFC 5322 message input; the operator parses no email/MIME.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. mime WordDecoder.DecodeHeader quadratic DoS requires untrusted MIME-encoded header input; the operator decodes no untrusted MIME.
govulncheck (binary mode) reports the vulnerable net/http/httputil ReverseProxy symbols are not reachable from this binary; the operator runs no reverse proxy.

containerd runAsNonRoot/user-ID-handling bypass affects the CRI container-runtime code path; the helm-operator vendors containerd transitively but is not a container runtime and never resolves container user IDs.

The operator's only network peer is the trusted in-cluster Kubernetes API server (and, for DNS, in-cluster CoreDNS); it accepts no connections from and sends no requests to attacker-controlled endpoints. OpenTelemetry-Go baggage-header DoS requires processing crafted inbound baggage headers; the operator's OTel use is outbound (its own telemetry) and exposes no endpoint parsing untrusted baggage.
OpenTelemetry-Go PATH-hijacking RCE affects only GOOS=bsd/solaris resource detectors; this image is GOOS=linux, so the vulnerable code path cannot execute.
